data.title: Shandong Humon Smelting Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Shandong Humon Smelting Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 76,444.28 million compared to CNY 58,160.6 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 76,444.28 million compared to CNY 58,160.6 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 561.57 million compared to CNY 464.53 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.49 compared to CNY 0.4 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.44 compared to CNY 0.34 a year ago.

data.title: Tranche Update on Lloyds Banking Group plc's Equity Buyback Plan announced on February 21, 2025.
data.description: From July 1, 2025 to September 30, 2025, the company has repurchased 800,000,000 shares, representing 1.33% for £667 million. With this, the company has completed the repurchase of 1,800,000,000 shares, representing 2.98% for £1,400 million under the buyback announced on February 21, 2025.

data.title: Roche Announces Appointment of Nina Schwab-Hautzinger as Head of Group Communications and Member of the Enlarged Corporate Executive Committee, Effective 1 February 2026
data.description:
Roche announced that Nina Schwab-Hautzinger (1975) has been appointed Head of Group Communications and a member of the enlarged Corporate Executive Committee effective 1 February 2026. Nina Schwab-Hautzinger rejoins Roche from her role at BASF as Head of Corporate Communications and Government Relations. During her previous thirteen year tenure at Roche, Nina Schwab-Hautzinger held various communications leadership roles such as Head of Corporate Brand & Communications at the Group level and Head of Communications & Public Affairs for Pharma in Germany.

She has also led the healthcare practice in a strategic communications consultancy in Singapore. Nina Schwab-Hautzinger studied at the University of Mannheim, Germany and the University of Waterloo, Canada. She received her doctorate from the University of Zurich, Switzerland, Department of Communication and Media Research.

data.title: Owners of utility-focused Conservice exploring sale at valuation as high as $5 billion, sources say
data.description:
NEW YORK (Reuters) -Advent International and TA Associates are exploring the sale of utility management and billing company Conservice that could value it at between $4.5 billion and $5 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

The private equity firms are working with investment bankers at Evercore on the sale, which is in its early stages, the people said, asking not to be named because the discussions are private. 

Advent declined to comment, while TA Associates, Evercore, and Conservice did not immediately respond to requests for comment. 

(Reporting by Milana Vinn in New York; Editing by David French, Dawn Kopecki and Cynthia Osterman)

By Milana Vinn

data.title: American International Group, Inc. Announces Appointment of Christophe Letondot as Head of Credit Lines
data.description: American International Group, Inc. announced the appointment of Christophe Letondot as head of credit lines. Letondot has more than three decades of international experience across insurance, banking, and capital markets. He will lead the firm's global Credit portfolio that includes Trade Credit, Trade Finance, Structured Credit and Political Risk businesses.

data.title: ViTrox Corporation Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
ViTrox Corporation Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was MYR 228.57 million compared to MYR 146.7 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 34.72 million compared to MYR 22.45 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0183 compared to MYR 0.0119 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0183 compared to MYR 0.0118 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was MYR 552.73 million compared to MYR 403.51 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 87.01 million compared to MYR 67.78 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.046 compared to MYR 0.0358 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0458 compared to MYR 0.0357 a year ago.

data.title: Security First Insurance Deploys Guidewire InsuranceNow to Modernize Operations
data.description: Security First Insurance and Guidewire announced that Security First deployed Guidewire InsuranceNow to modernize its operations to enhance agent and customer service. The company implemented InsuranceNow to its homeowners and dwelling fire lines of business simultaneously.

data.title: Hexaware Appoints Eravi Gopan as President and Global Head ? High Tech, Products, and Platforms
data.description:
Hexaware Technologies announced the appointment of Eravi Gopan as President and Global Head ? High Tech, Products, and Platforms. He will lead Hexaware's global strategy, growth, and client success initiatives across the vertical. With deep experience in SaaS, AI, and technology services, Eravi is recognized for building and scaling businesses across geographies and industry verticals.

He has led high-growth teams in venture-funded Silicon Valley start-ups, large global enterprises, and partnered deeply with foundational technology companies. He served as Chief Revenue Officer at a Google Ventures-backed SaaS platform, where he led a successful transformation and acquisition, and more recently as the first Chief Commercial Officer of an Accel Ventures-backed enterprise AI startup. A mentor at the Founder Institute, Silicon Valley and an advisor to multiple startups, Eravi previously spent a long tenure at Wipro, where he built several first-time businesses across diverse industry verticals.

He was part of the senior leadership team that scaled Wipro's global technology business and, in his last role, served as Global Business Head for Platforms and Software Products. Eravi holds degrees in electronics engineering and business management and lives in the San Francisco Bay Area.

S&P cuts India's Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles outlook on slow Jaguar Land Rover recovery
data.description:
(Reuters) -Ratings agency S&P Global said on Thursday it is downgrading the outlook of India's Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles to 'negative' on "slow recovery" of its UK unit, Jaguar Land Rover, which was hit by a cyber attack earlier this year.

(Reporting by Hritam Mukherjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber)

HSBC Puts Egypt Retail Business Under Review Amid Global Overhaul
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud


HSBC Holdings plans to review strategic options for its retail banking business in Egypt, as the lender moves to scale back some operations as part of an overhaul of its global footprint.

Europe's biggest bank by assets has been seeking to streamline operations and cut costs since Chief Executive Georges Elhedery took the helm last year.

The U.K.-based, Asia-focused lender said Thursday that the review of its Egyptian business would center on its retail banking operations only, leaving out wholesale banking activities.

The move follows similar strategic reviews the group opened earlier this year for its retail businesses in Australia, Indonesia and Sri Lanka. Elhedery said in an earnings call in July that another review, in Bangladesh, had concluded and would result in the wind-down of its retail business there in the second half of the year.


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1107ET
